Motion To Compel Response To Request For Identification And Production Of Documents (Set Two)
Off Calendar Feb-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Claude Wyle, Esq., CHOULOS, CHOULOS & WYLE, LLP (415) 474-7800 Attorneys for Plaintiff Oscar Padgett.
Giuliana F. Regina, Esq. FORD, WALKER, HAGGERTY & BEHAR (562) 983-2500 gregina@fwhb.com Attorneys for Defendants CARL ANDREW CASTRO, HERTZ VEHICLES LLC (sued erroneously herein as HERTZ VEHICLES, LLC) and THE HERTZ CORPORATION

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts the tentative ruling as modifeid:

DEFENDANT CARL CASTRO, THE HERTZ CORPORATION, HERTZ VEHICLES LLC (SUED ERRONEOUSLY HEREIN AS Motion To Compel Response To Request For Identification And Production Of Documents (Set Two)

Granted, in part. RFP No. 25 seeks potentially relevant information and is limited in scope to the date of the incident. Plaintiff raises a privacy objection but offers no facts to support the objection. Counsel for the parties shall meet and confer in good faith within 5 days to establish a protocol for Plaintiff to provide the video requested by RFP No. 25 to Defendant for joint inspection and in a manner that ensures the evidence is preserved whereby Defendant shall bear any costs related to inspection or recovery of information from the video, provide Plaintiff with a copy of any information recovered for a "first look," and allow Plaintiff a reasonable opportunity to assert any objections and/or seek a protective order, if necessary.

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in copliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
